qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: We wish to schedule three processes , and on a uniprocessor system. The priorities, CPU time requirements and arrival times of the processes are as shown below. We have a choice of preemptive or non-preemptive scheduling. In preemptive scheduling, a late-arriving higher priority process can preempt

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: In the working-set strategy, which of the following is done by the operating system to prevent thrashing? I. It initiates another process if there are enough extra frames. II. It selects a process to suspend if the sum of the sizes of the working-sets exceeds the total number of available frames. A.
